text.content:
What this module does

Substances and Paraphernalia ships ready-to-use illicit substances (consumables) and the paraphernalia required to consume them. When a substance with required paraphernalia is used through dnd5e's Activity flow, the module checks whether the actor has the required gear in a ready state and either allows the use or shows a Missing paraphernalia dialog with a Use anyway override.

How gating works

Each substance carries a list of paraphernalia requirement groups in its module flags. The outer list is AND (every group must be satisfied); each group's anyOf list is OR (any single referenced paraphernalia satisfies that group). References can be either compendium UUIDs (stable, recommended for shipped content) or kebab-case slugs that match a paraphernaliaId flag on the actor's items (the author-friendly path).

Paraphernalia must be ready:

  • Equipment (e.g. a pipe) must be equipped.
  • Consumable paraphernalia (e.g. rolling papers) must have quantity > 0.
  • Attunement-required paraphernalia must be attuned on the actor's copy.

The dialog names the substance and the missing groups, with reasons annotated — “(not equipped)” or “(not attuned)” — when at least one candidate is on the actor but unready. Every user (GM or player) sees the same dialog with the same buttons; the player override is intentional and mirrors how dnd5e surfaces missing-resource overrides for spells, Lay on Hands, and the like. Abuse is a social problem the GM handles at the table.

Authoring your own substances and paraphernalia

The full flag schema lives in docs/flag-schema.md in the module distribution. The short version:

flags["substances-and-paraphernalia"] = {
  kind: "substance" | "paraphernalia",
  setting: "fantasy" | "sciFi" | "modern",
  category: "stimulant" | "mindAltering" | "performanceEnhancing", // substance
  paraphernaliaId: "kebab-case-slug",                              // paraphernalia
  requiredParaphernalia: [{ anyOf: ["slug-or-Compendium.UUID"] }], // substance
  administration: "inhaled"|"ingested"|"injected"|"sublingual"|"topical", // substance
  addiction: { save: { ability: "con", dc: 13 }, withdrawalMod: 4, addictionEffectId: "<ae._id>" }, // substance
  addictionSaveBypass: { type: "auto-pass", appliesTo: ["inhaled"], usesPerDay: "@prof" }, // paraphernalia (optional)
  schemaVersion: 2
};

For substances to consume and self-destruct correctly, the substance's Activity must have a Consumption target of type Item Uses with value 1, and the item's Uses must be configured with max: 1 and autoDestroy: true.

Limitations

The gate only fires for substances used through the dnd5e Activity flow (dnd5e.preUseActivity). RP-style direct inventory edits, custom macros that bypass Activities, or right-click → Use outside the Activity flow will bypass paraphernalia gating. This is intentional at v0.1; if a player consumes a substance through a back channel, the GM handles it at the table.

Optional integrations

The module knows about four optional Foundry modules. None are required.

  • Dynamic Active Effects (dae) — required for substances whose Active Effects use DAE-only modes. Those effects carry requiresDae: true; without DAE active, a warning is shown at use time. The world setting Block substances that require DAE when DAE is missing can promote the warning to a hard block.
  • Midi-QoL — automated saves and chained workflow effects. Deeper integration is post-MVP.
  • Times Up — reliable Active Effect expiration on durations.
  • Token Magic FX — visual filters tied to substance effects. Post-MVP.

To hide the ready-time notice listing missing-but-recommended modules, enable the client setting Suppress integration warnings.

Settings reference

  • Enforce paraphernalia requirements (world, default on) — master switch for gating.
  • Block substances that require DAE when DAE is missing (world, default off) — promotes DAE warning to a hard block.
  • Suppress integration warnings (client, default off) — hides the ready-time integration notice.
  • Debug logging (client, default off) — verbose console logging.

The included macro Toggle Paraphernalia Enforcement flips the master switch from the hotbar.

Troubleshooting

  • The dialog doesn't appear when I expect it. Confirm the substance has flags["substances-and-paraphernalia"].kind = "substance" and a non-empty requiredParaphernalia. Confirm Enforce paraphernalia requirements is on. Confirm the substance is being used via an Activity (the chat-card Use button on the item sheet), not a manual chat message.
  • Override fires but the substance still won't auto-destroy. Open the activity's Consumption tab and confirm there is an Item Uses target with value 1. Without it the activity runs but never increments uses, so autoDestroy never fires.
  • Effect appears applied but nothing seems to happen. The effect may use a DAE-only mode. Confirm DAE is active and that the effect carries requiresDae: true; if so, that's the warning the module is trying to surface.

Addiction & Withdrawal

Each substance is tuned along two axes: an addictiveness DC (Constitution save by default) and a Withdrawal Modifier (an integer that drives how long withdrawal lasts). After a substance is used, the module rolls the save through dnd5e's standard save dialog. On a failed save, the actor gains an Active Effect cloned from a template AE that lives on the substance item.

Withdrawal duration on a fresh failure is max(WMod − Constitution modifier, ⌈WMod/2⌉), with a minimum of 1 long rest. The result is stored on the actor as flags["substances-and-paraphernalia"].withdrawal[<substanceId>] = { restsRemaining, appliedAt } — that flag is the canonical state. The Active Effect on the actor is a UI mirror of the flag and carries flags["substances-and-paraphernalia"].sourceSubstanceId so the long-rest tick can match the AE back to its flag entry.

Re-using a substance you're already addicted to does not roll a new save; it extends withdrawal to max(currentRestsRemaining, newComputed). The intent is that bingeing while addicted can prolong, but cannot shorten, an existing withdrawal.

The long-rest tick is fired by dnd5e.restCompleted with longRest: true. To prevent multi-client double-ticks, only the active GM (game.users.activeGM) decrements. Each entry's restsRemaining drops by 1; on reaching zero, the matching AE is removed and the flag entry cleared. Short rests do nothing.

Active Effect naming convention: addiction templates are named {Substance} Addiction. The substring addict (case-insensitive) must appear in the AE name. The substance's addictionEffectId flag points to the template AE's _id; macros and the Remove Addiction tool prefer that pointer but fall back to the name match for hand-edited content.

The Remove Addiction macro (GM-only) gathers candidate AEs on the selected token by name match, by sourceSubstanceId, and by matching withdrawal flag entry, then offers a per-AE checkbox preview. On confirm it whispers the removed AE JSON to the GM (paste-restore safety net), deletes the AEs, and clears the matching flag entries.

Save Bypass & Administration

Substances declare an administration mode (inhaled, ingested, injected, sublingual, or topical). Paraphernalia may optionally declare an addictionSaveBypass, which lets that paraphernalia auto-pass addiction saves on substances whose administration matches.

// on a paraphernalia item:
flags["substances-and-paraphernalia"].addictionSaveBypass = {
  type: "auto-pass",          // v2 supports only auto-pass; advantage / +N reserved
  appliesTo: ["inhaled"],     // matches substance.administration
  usesPerDay: "@prof"          // numeric or formula; cosmetic — see below
};

Per-day uses ride on dnd5e's native item recovery. Bypass-granting paraphernalia ship with system.uses.max set to the same formula and system.uses.recovery: [{ period: "day", type: "recoverAll" }]. The module decrements system.uses.spent when a bypass fires; dnd5e refreshes spent on long rest. The module does not implement a custom recovery hook for paraphernalia.

Important rule: a bypass only triggers when the granting paraphernalia is one of the gate-satisfying items for that substance — i.e. its slug or compendium UUID appears in the substance's requiredParaphernalia. Attuning a bypass-granter does nothing for substances that don't name it. This is intentional: the GM controls which substances a bypass-granter applies to by writing it into the substance's requirements.

Worked example: Shen Feng Wa's Dubious Pipe (legendary, attuned) declares addictionSaveBypass: { type: "auto-pass", appliesTo: ["inhaled"], usesPerDay: "@prof" }. Burning Wa's Reserve (a substance whose requiredParaphernalia includes the pipe by compendium UUID) auto-bypasses up to PB Constitution saves per day; pipe spent increments per use; long rest resets it. Burning Coalshade Powder while attuned to the pipe does not bypass — Coalshade's requirements name the Snuff Horn, not the pipe.

Refusal cases (the save rolls normally): the substance has no administration; the substance has no required paraphernalia; no gate-satisfying paraphernalia grants a matching bypass; matching paraphernalia exist but are out of uses for the day.
